skill.name: Security Auditor
skill.description: Expert security auditor specializing in DevSecOps, comprehensive cybersecurity, and compliance frameworks.
skill.summary: Comprehensive security auditing skill for vulnerability assessment, threat modeling, and compliance implementation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing security auditing guidance and knowledge. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or external commands detected. The skill provides expert security knowledge without any security risks.

content.user_title: Perform expert security audits and compliance assessments
content.value_statement: Organizations struggle to integrate security into development workflows and meet compliance requirements. This skill provides comprehensive security auditing expertise including DevSecOps integration, vulnerability assessment, and regulatory compliance guidance.
actual_capabilities: Conduct comprehensive security audits of applications and infrastructure
Implement DevSecOps practices with SAST, DAST, and dependency scanning in CI/CD
Perform threat modeling using STRIDE, PASTA, and attack tree methodologies
Guide compliance implementation for GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, and ISO 27001
Design secure authentication systems with OAuth 2.0, OIDC, and MFA
Create incident response plans with forensics and breach notification procedures
limitations: Cannot execute automated security scans or penetration tests directly
Requires manual review and validation of all security recommendations
Does not replace formal compliance certification or legal counsel
Security testing scope must be approved before intrusive assessments

use_cases.title: Security Audit for Microservices
use_cases.description: Conduct comprehensive security assessment of microservices architecture including API security, container security, and DevSecOps pipeline integration.

use_cases.title: Compliance Readiness Assessment
use_cases.description: Prepare for SOC 2, GDPR, or HIPAA compliance with gap analysis, control implementation guidance, and audit preparation documentation.

use_cases.title: Threat Modeling Workshop
use_cases.description: Facilitate structured threat modeling sessions using STRIDE methodology to identify and prioritize security risks in new application designs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Security Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code snippet for security vulnerabilities. Identify any OWASP Top 10 issues, suggest fixes, and explain the security impact of each finding.

prompt_templates.title: DevSecOps Pipeline Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a security scanning pipeline for our CI/CD workflow. Include SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, and container image scanning with specific tool recommendations and integration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Threat Modeling Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform threat modeling for our application using STRIDE methodology. The application architecture includes: [describe architecture]. Identify threats, assess risk levels, and recommend mitigations for each threat category.

prompt_templates.title: Compliance Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a gap analysis against [GDPR/HIPAA/SOC 2/ISO 27001] requirements. Review our current controls: [describe controls]. Identify gaps, prioritize remediation efforts, and provide implementation guidance for each requirement.

output_examples.input: Security audit request for REST API with JWT authentication
output: Critical: JWT tokens stored in localStorage - vulnerable to XSS. Recommendation: Use httpOnly cookies with SameSite=Strict
High: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoints - enables brute force. Recommendation: Implement rate limiting with exponential backoff
Medium: No input validation on user registration - potential injection. Recommendation: Add schema validation and sanitization
Low: Missing security headers. Recommendation: Add CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options headers

output_examples.input: DevSecOps pipeline design for Node.js application
output: Build Stage: npm audit, Snyk dependency scanning, SonarQube SAST
Test Stage: OWASP ZAP DAST scan, container image scanning with Trivy
Deploy Stage: OPA policy validation, Kubernetes admission controls
Post-deploy: Continuous monitoring with Falco runtime security

best_practices: Integrate security scanning early in the development lifecycle to catch vulnerabilities before they reach production
Apply defense-in-depth principles with multiple security layers rather than relying on single controls
Automate security validation in CI/CD pipelines to ensure consistent security enforcement across deployments
anti_patterns: Running intrusive security tests in production environments without written authorization and proper safeguards
Storing secrets in environment variables or configuration files instead of using dedicated secrets management solutions
Treating automated scan results as complete security validation without manual review and context analysis

faq.question: Can this skill perform automated security scans?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance and recommendations but cannot execute automated scanning tools directly. Use it to design scanning strategies, interpret results, and implement remediation.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for formal compliance certification?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance for compliance implementation but does not replace formal certification processes. Use it to prepare for audits, implement controls, and understand requirements. Engage qualified auditors for official certification.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers major frameworks including GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC 2, ISO 27001, and NIST Cybersecurity Framework. It can help implement controls and prepare documentation for these standards.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for penetration testing?
faq.answer: This skill can guide penetration testing methodology, help interpret findings, and recommend remediation. However, actual penetration testing should be performed by qualified professionals with proper authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes, the security principles and methodologies apply across languages. The skill can provide language-specific guidance for secure coding practices in popular languages including JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, and others.

faq.question: How often should I run security audits with this skill?
faq.answer: Security audits should be integrated continuously into development. Run security reviews for each significant feature, perform comprehensive audits quarterly, and conduct threat modeling during architecture design phases.
